full_text:
SOP for meetings

General guidelines

For thesis committees and meetings with external collaborations, Remember to send Outlook calendar invitations (with zoom/teams link).

Every project (and thus the project meetings too) needs a clear project lead. The project lead chairs meetings and prepares and shares the agenda for every meeting in advance. It is also the duty of every attendee to read the agenda in advance to use the meeting time effectively.

The agenda should include clear information on the aim and contents of the meeting, so everyone can understand the progression of the meeting

The agenda can be posted to the slack channel of the project, or the calendar booking in Google Calendar, as long as everyone attending the meeting knows where to find the agenda

Mark in Google calendar what the topic is and who is joining. Please use complete words/sentences that describe the goal of the meeting - this makes it possible for everyone to prepare For example - NNN project meeting to check results of XXX and decide YYYY- Anniina, Ada, Joonas

When chairing a meeting:

Lead the discussion through the agenda prepared and shared in advance - make sure you track the time

First 5-10 minutes are used to summarise the big picture, overall goals of the meeting and to present the agenda

Last 5-10 minutes are used to

recap the conclusions

make sure everyone is aligned with the action items and understand their own tasks

book the next meeting if needed

Booking a meeting with Anniina

Book in advance

It is easiest to find free slots within 1-2 weeks

It is encouraged to book the next meeting in the end of the previous meeting

Check calendars

Lab common and Anniina’s own for tentative slots

Anniina’s typical working hours are from 9-17 - if needed you can ask for exceptions

No more than 6 meetings per day - if urgent you can ask for exceptions

The max duration of effective meetings is 55min (1h slot) - ask personally for exceptions. Confirm the slot with Anniina using Slack private message.

To set up doodles for e.g. Thesis committee book tentative slots from Anniina’s and then generate the doodle

No more than 1x1h tentative slots per day

Spread the the slots over min 3 days and 2 weeks - this allows more flexibility for busy PIs

After the doodle is finalised, remove the tentative slots
